observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant commented that reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accounting Office and General Services Office is on-going. Copies of Journal Entry Vouchers for the reclassification of the identified misclassified items totaling P1,612,400.00 were likewise furnished to the Audit Team.
She then explained that as per records of their office, the Paging System amounting to P99,540.00 is under the Market Fund and is booked under Communication Equipment. But she stated that upon clarification with the OIC-Municipal GSO, they will make the necessary update in their records.
observations.reason: Reconciled discrepancies totaling to P16,570,782.86 was noted in CY 2020 audit. The Municipal GSO was able to submit an updated IIRUP. The Municipal GSO is preparing necessary and reasonable documents/reports for disposal and is acting on the properties to be disposed within reasonable time. Review of the Physical Count of PPE by type as of given date and fully accomplished all the columns for the prescribed format; the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for the Municipality is being used. Appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties as required by PD No. 1445 to prevent exposure to elements and further deterioration of values is yet to be conducted. The cause of the negative and small amount of payable accounts is not yet identified; analysis and reclassification/adjustments are being made by the Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting Officer to submit a written justification for non-disposition/divestment of Talisay Public Market as well as that of other unserviceable property and other property plant and equipment to the Commission. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit JEVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for review and appraisal, contrary to Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445, pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995, Section 167 and of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005, thus casting doubt on the regularity and validity of derecognitions made from the Municipality’s Property Plant and Equipment account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant replied that Full payment of retiring employees and terminal leave benefits of its retiring employees is properly being implemented. Also, the Municipal Accountant requires the proper classification to be used for the transactions in the General Fund book for the transactions pertaining to the said misclassified accounts in order to fairly present the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accounting Office is observing the necessary requirements prior to the demolition of properties pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386 and PD No. 1445. Reconciliations and documents were submitted; derecognitions from the accounts are now in compliance.
Reiterated in finding no. 3 (CY 2020). The practice was observed again in CY 2020 audit.

observations.managementAction: The Petty Cash Fund (PCF) Custodian replied that the replenishment of the PCF is being done if its utilization already reaches 75 per cent. The amount of the Municipality’s PCF is P50,000.00.
Coordination with DepED personnel was made. The collection of receivables is being pursued and individual letters/demands are to be sent. No legal actions were initiated during the year.
observations.reason: Taal Volcano eruption and COVID-19 pandemic affected the full implementation of the agency’s action plan. Reconciliation and collection are on-going; no legal action taken yet.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt additional measures and strategies to intensify the collection of taxes and generate more income; coordinate with the barangays in the implementation of Real Property Tax Program to maximize collection of taxes and continuously coordinate with the Provincial Government and Sangguniang Panlalawigan to avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Statement of Account (SOA) for unpaid real property taxes has been delivered to individual taxpayers residing outside the Municipality. Coordination with Provincial Government and Sangguniang Panlalawigan to avail remedies was made.
observations.reason: Taal Volcano eruption and COVID-19 pandemic affected full implementation. The SOA has been delivered; coordination with provincial offices has been made.

observations.recommendation:
a. Release the retention money to the concerned suppliers in accordance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
b. Conduct a thorough review and analysis and ascertain the cause of the negative and small balances of this payable accounts and effect the necessary reclassification or adjustments. Attach relevant documents and analysis of the accounts to the JEVs for any adjustment to be made.
c. Henceforth carefully check, analyze, review and promptly record the transactions to arrive at an accurate and correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reviewed the accounts and reclassified necessary transactions. The Municipal Office analyzed the accounts and is preparing adjustments. Reconciliations being made; relevant documents and analysis will be attached to JEVs for adjustments required.
observations.reason: The retention money has not been released in some cases. Review and analysis are being made by the Office; some documentary deficiencies remain to be resolved.

observations.recommendation:
a. Municipal Officials to give priority to effect the full payment of retiring employees Terminal Leave Benefits of its retiring employees.
b. Municipal Accountant to use proper classification to record the transactions correctly.
observations.managementAction: Full payment of retiring employees and terminal leave benefits of its retiring employees is prioritized. Proper accounts classification is now being used.

observations.recommendation:
a. Implement measures to increase the income and decrease the expenses of the Operation of Tourism to ensure that it will sufficiently meet or even exceed its operating costs to help sustain the Municipality’s operation; and
b. Study the viability of this enterprise and submit plans for the improvement of its collections as well as measures to decrease its expenses to ensure its sustainability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Office-in-Charge implements measures to improve collections and is studying the viability of tourism operation. Management is establishing plans for restoration and improvement of collections; implementation is on-going.
observations.reason: Taal Volcano eruption and COVID-19 pandemic affected full implementation of the action plan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer to ensure that the LDRRMFIP contains the details of the projects and activities to be funded, including those planned for the utilization of the previous year’s LDRRMF so that the Municipality becomes a disaster resilient community; and to accomplish annually the LDRRMFIP by providing sufficient information in the listed PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Management is finalizing the projects and activities planned for the utilization of the previous year’s LDRRMF. The Municipality is updating the LDRRMFIP to include details of projects and activities to be funded.
observations.reason: Details of the projects and activities to be funded are being prepared; not yet presented in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
a. Decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended QRF and MOOE; and
b. Accomplish completely the LDRRMFIP by providing sufficient information in the listed PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is finalizing the consolidation of funds and planning PPAs. The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) is making adjustments to the LDRRMFIP and planning of PPAs.
observations.reason: The Municipality is finalizing LDRRMFIP details; projects and activities to be funded are not yet presented in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point conducts regular monitoring on implementation of GAD Plan and Budget. Establishment of GAD Database and maintenance of sex-disaggregated data is on-going.
observations.reason: GAD database establishment is on-going. Lack of submission of GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report prevented full evaluation (finding no. 15).

observations.recommendation: c. GSO to take immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the abovementioned unserviceable properties as required by PD No. 1445 to prevent exposure to different elements and further deterioration of its values; d. Creation of a Committee to reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPEs with the property and accounting records; Prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed format and submit the IIRUP for the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GSO was able to submit an updated IIRUP. A staff was assigned to reconcile the count of all PPEs with the property and accounting records. Appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties is yet to be conducted.
observations.reason: Appraisal and disposal of unserviceable properties has not been conducted; reconciliation of physical count with property and accounting records is on-going.
Reiterated in finding no. 3 for CY 2020; similar observation noted in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
a. stop the practice of giving financial assistance directly to the school/school heads; instead consolidate the request of the school heads for preparation of Program of Work and budget. If it falls under the SEF, procurement should be through proper RA No. 9184 mode;
d. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary reclassification and adjustments in the General Fund book for the said misclassified transactions in order to fairly present the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No financial assistance was directly given to the school/school heads for CY 2020. The Municipal Accountant prepared necessary reclassifications in the General Fund transactions to reflect proper classification.

observations.recommendation: a. regularly reconcile accounting records with the GSIS records in order to ascertain the reliability of the Due to GSIS account and the amount due; b. exert effort to remit the correct contributions and follow RA No. 8291 provisions; and c. coordinate with the Municipal Human Resource and Management (HRM) Office for supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accounting Office and GSIS is on-going. The Municipal Accountant reviews monthly deductions and remittances. Analysis being done to address the origin of the negative balances.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is on-going; negative balances originated in prior years and further analysis is being conducted.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor enjoin the concerned offices and the management to refrain from resorting to procurement and disbursement through reimbursement basis; all payments should be made directly to the supplier/contractor by check in order for the municipality to deduct and withhold applicable taxes and remit the same in accordance with BIR regulations.
observations.managementAction: Procurements are now being made through proper procurement methods and payments are being processed by check. The accounting office requires supporting documents in prescribed forms before processing claims.
observations.reason: Some procurements are now based on proper procurement modes; some documentary deficiencies remain for certain transactions.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer to submit relevant documents and explanations and to strictly and closely monitor the construction; b. Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents required under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing payments; c. issue notice of suspension and demand for liquidated damages from erring contractors for delayed completion of work and submit all the lacking documents to the Audit Team to avoid suspension/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Some of the required documents were not submitted. The Municipal Accounting Office reviews transactions and supporting documents before payment and the Municipal Engineer is monitoring the construction. Some documentary deficiencies were identified and being worked on.
observations.reason: Additional deficiencies were noted by COA during ocular inspection and Special Audit. Some supporting documents remain unsubmitted; hence Notice of Suspension was issued in Special Audit.

observations.recommendation: a. The Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and TWG to provide explanation for inconsistencies and ensure winning bidders submit performance securities within prescribed period; b. require winning bidders to submit performance securities with validity periods; c. BAC Secretariat to ensure posting of warranty securities and follow-up for liquidated damages; d. Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents prior to payment; e. BAC to ensure transparency and compliance with procurement laws.
observations.managementAction: All infrastructure projects are being monitored by the Municipal Engineer. Some lacking documents were submitted. Winning bidders are now being required to submit performance securities within validity periods. BAC and TWG are reviewing documents more strictly.
observations.reason: Some documentary deficiencies still noted and included in Special Audit; some required submissions are on-going.

observations.recommendation: a. Scrutinize sufficiency of winning bidder’s manpower and equipment on all projects to be awarded and not awarded on individual infrastructure project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or overlapping implementation period, requiring the winning bidder to supplement manpower and/or equipment or disqualify it from obtaining several awards; b. Municipal Engineer and MPDO to strictly and closely monitor all projects and prepare status reports per project for monitoring.
observations.managementAction: All infrastructure projects are being monitored by the Municipal Engineer; submission of status reports is being required. Technical and financial documents in terms of manpower and equipment are now being evaluated.
observations.reason: Status reports were not submitted for all projects; some documentary deficiencies remain.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor enjoin the BAC to recommend appropriate method of procurement and encourage public bidding rather than alternative modes when circumstances allow and avoid splitting of purchases to promote competition and transparency. Require the Accounting Office to ensure supporting documents for disbursements are duly and properly filled-up.
observations.managementAction: Procurements are now based on appropriate methods; accounting office requires supporting documents and proper filling-up before processing claims. Some supporting documents remain lacking for certain transactions.
observations.reason: Some procurements are now appropriately processed; however, documentary deficiencies remain for certain disbursements.

observations.recommendation: a. Ensure allocation for projects related to disaster mitigation and preparedness are included in the LDRRMFIP; b. meticulously plan specific programs and projects necessary in prevention, mitigation and preparedness; c. carefully scrutinize expenses charged to the fund to avoid disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Projects are being planned and LDRRMFIP is being updated; expenses for CY 2020 were related to disaster response.

observations.recommendation:
a) Establish GAD Database and update sex-disaggregated data as required under JMC No. 2013-01; b) GAD Focal Person to properly monitor and report regularly the implementation of GAD activities; c) utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities.
We also recommended preparation of annual GAD Accomplishment Report in required format for proper evaluation.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Point conducts monitoring and is establishing the GAD Database; implementation of GAD activities is monitored. GAD Accomplishment Report for the year under audit was not submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The GAD Database establishment is on-going; GAD Accomplishment Report for the year under audit not submitted (finding no. 15).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to: 

Direct the City Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method for supplies and materials; and 

Direct the General Services Officer to establish control procedures to regulate the issuance of supplies to actual requirements of end-users within a month.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.
Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Treasurer to explain in writing the incurrence of negative balance of the Cash in Bank amounting to ₱52,120,621.89.

We also recommended that Management direct the City Treasurer to observe proper cash management to reduce the risk of inability to meet immediate cash requirements of the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct  the City Accountant to determine the cause of negative balances and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in order to correct the negative balances of the CIP account.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify the line items amounting to ₱121,551,422.44 to their proper account/special account classifications.
observations.managementAction: Management made the necessary reclassifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to take up in the agency’s books the total amount of ₱29,360,762.70 under Receivables-Disallowances/Charges account which is the total disallowance in COA Order of Execution (COE) No. 2015-179.
observations.managementAction: The receivables were already taken up in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Panlungsod, adopt and implement guidelines taking into consideration the following:

Review the amounts of cell card load allowance in relation to the available landlines/mobile phones and internet connection and consider its reduction.

The officials/employees who should be entitled to it and the amount one can claim for his official phone calls and any excess on the limit set, shall be for the personal account of the user.

If cell cards shall be allowed, consider the use of mobile phone fixed plan offered by telecommunications network.
observations.managementAction: Management formulated guidelines on the grant of cellular card allowances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the BAC to explain in writing why the Abstract of Canvass/Bids were signed by the canvassing officer and the head of the requesting department instead them; and

Require a written explanation from the canvassing officer and the head of the requesting department why the contracts were awarded before the deadline of the submission of bids/RFQs.
observations.managementAction: Written explanation was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the DepEd – Talisay City Division to explain in writing why the specifications of the books requested for the Kindergarten and Grade 1 pupils did not comply with the DepEd DO No. 16, s.2016; and

Review carefully the submitted Purchase Requests from various end-users to avoid unnecessary expenditures of public funds.
observations.managementAction: Written justification was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to: 

a. Trace and identify the causes of the unreconciled balances of the reciprocal accounts and effect their reconciliation; and 

b. Maintain and update regularly the subsidiary ledgers of the said accounts to facilitate early detection of errors, and determine if the total of the individual accounts tally with the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: The reciprocal accounts were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Sangguniang Panlungsod and the Office of the City Assessor to have proper coordination to resolve the impasse regarding the general revision of the real property assessments in order to update the assessed value of the real property within the City’s jurisdiction and consequently increase its revenue/income.
observations.managementAction: The City already implemented the revision of real property assessments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Make a demand to CSWMI to comply with the material provisions in the contract in order to establish a controlled sanitary landfill, otherwise, rescind the contract; and

Direct the Ecological Solid Waste Management Office to make a regular monitoring on the actual implementation of the contract.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Reconsider the JVA and determine whether the failure of CSWMI in the performance of its obligations constitutes material breach in the agreement and pursue necessary legal actions; and

Compel CSMI to perform its obligations, if warranted, under the JVA in order for the City to receive the benefits therefrom.
We recommended that Management install CCTV cameras in the City Treasurer’s Office to deter robberies, theft, and irregularities of cash and cash items thus safeguarding the assets of the agency.
observations.managementAction: CCTVs were already installed in the offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the City Engineer to submit a justification and explanation why the contract was reported as 100% complete despite the noted variance;

Remind and require the City Engineer to be more circumspect in the inspection of actual accomplishment of projects considering that it is his mandated duty to administer, coordinate, supervise, and control the construction, maintenance, improvement, and repair of roads, bridges, and other engineering and public works projects of the local government unit as provided in Section 477 of the Local Government Code; and

Require the contractor to correct the noted deficiency; otherwise, settle the deficiency in the amount of ₱10,758.00 as it constitutes irregular expenditure, hence disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Refund was already made by the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to achieve completeness of documentary requirements, economy and effectiveness in the implementation of infrastructure projects and to avoid suspension and eventual disallowance of the transaction.

We also recommended that Management submit the above specified documentary requirements or a written explanation why such could not be submitted.
observations.managementAction: Required supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of the LGC and to transfer/realign any existing appropriations charged to the LAFF in the SP Office to the budget of the Mayor’s Office.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the practice.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer religiously submit to the COA auditor, copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO, the annual LDRRMFIP and monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund certified correct by the City Accountant pursuant to items 5.1.5 and 5/1/6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the LDRRMFIP prepared in accordance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The proper format was already used by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require all transfer of funds to Barangays to implement the projects and programs of the City be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement and other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish proper accountability and monitoring of the proper utilization of the funds. 

Moreover, require the officials of the barangays concerned for the immediate liquidation of the amounts granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the City.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory taking of all the PPE items to ascertain the actual existence and condition of the properties and equipment. The committee may be composed of personnel from Accounting and General Services Office to work together to reconcile both the inventory records and the property ledger cards.
direct the City Accountant to re-classify unserviceable properties to Other Assets awaiting for their disposal if there are any based on the inventory report of the committee;
exert efforts to locate the missing properties and make a demand to the end users for the return of the missing items.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.
Management has started to work on the titling of the lots but discrepancies in the documents have delayed the processing of titles.

observations.recommendation:
Collect from the retired personnel their outstanding liabilities;
Require the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable including those who signed the clearance of retired personnel in satisfaction of the disallowances or charges as mandated under COA Circular 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Only the amount of ₱3,712.00 was collected.
Management is still in the process of migrating data from the old system to the eNGAS.

Moreover, require the officials of the barangays concerned for the immediate liquidation of the amounts granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to create two plantilla positions for a) administrative and training; b) research and planning and hire qualified personnel to occupy these positions following the qualification standards, position titles and salary grades set by NDRRMC, DIL, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management facilitate the completion of the 10-year solid waste management plan.  We also recommended that, Management comply with all applicable rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing the open dump site, develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and eventually, residual wastes of the City.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Schools Division Superintendent to submit the following:

Explanation/justification on the procurement of SRMs that are not included in the list of approved Supplementary Reading Materials;
Consolidated List of Requested SRMs of the Division pursuant to Item 3 of DepEd Order No. 53 which shall be supported by the following:
School Inventory of SRMs
List of usage of new SRMs needed by the school
How the SRMs that will be procured will support the curriculum implementation; and
Approval of the request from the Office of the DepEd Secretary.

b. Direct the BAC to submit an explanation on why the procurements of SRMs were made without considering the provisions of DepEd Order No. 53 s. 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management use the e-NGAS because of the aforementioned features which are not available in the system currently being used by the Accounting Office.

We also recommended that Management:

Hire encoders, preferably with Accounting background, to speed up the data build-up and beginning balances, as well as the encoding of transactions to the System, until a parallel test shall have been conducted; and 

Provide the Accounting Office with a separate server to ensure the efficiency of the System and to safeguard its database.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to establish an Internal Audit Service office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445 in order to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, improve the quality and quantity of outputs and enable agencies to better respond to the requirements of the public they serve.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management avail of the cash card services offered by the Development Bank of the Philippines or Landbank of the Philippines in the payroll distribution of the job order workers or formulate and implement strategies to minimize the waiting time of the job order workers by observing a schedule for the distribution of the salary per office.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the different market collectors to prepare, on a periodic basis, the Report on Unpaid Stall Rentals and submit to the City Treasurer/Accountant for monitoring/recording  purposes; and

Require the City Accountant that the total realizable income from market stall rental fees is properly taken up in the books of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.
Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Accountant to record in the individual employees’ ledgers the amount of contributions/deductions and reconcile the accounts and explain why the amount remitted differed from the amount withheld thus the incurrence of over remittance to GSIS and non- remittance to BIR, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth of material amounts.

Remit regularly all amounts due to the BIR within the prescribed time to avoid payment of penalties, surcharges and interest arising from violations and preclude delay in the processing of members’/employees claims and present the account correctly in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.
Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the City Treasurer to employ more aggressive collection strategies such as tax mapping, monitoring of weights, calibration of fuel meters, and other means to increase collections.

Establish an Internal Audit Service office and install a sound system of internal control to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, improve the quality and quantity of outputs and enable agencies to better respond to the requirements of the public they serve in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.

Conduct an impartial investigation and institute appropriate sanctions against those personnel found responsible in the commission of such unlawful act.  
Direct the officials/employees found liable be made to refund the amount of ₱7,272,750.00.
Remind the Technical Working Group (TWG) to review the canvass documents carefully to promote fairness and transparency in the procurement process and obtain reasonable price offers most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: A case had been filed with OMB-Visayas.

observations.recommendation: Direct that the officials/employees found liable for the falsification of the public documents to immediately refund the amount of ₱2,195,000.00.
observations.managementAction: A case had been filed with OMB-V.

Not complied by Management.  
Pertains to previous administration.

observations.recommendation: Deduct the amount of ₱319,203.72, from the remaining claim of Automated Technologies, Inc. representing excessive cost of the contract price, otherwise the amount will be disallowed in audit.

observations.managementAction:
The party to whom payment was erroneously made continued to insist that they were the rightful owners while one said that she cannot refund because the money had already been spent by her.

Management intends to pursue the actions taken by a previous Legal Officer regarding the matter.

observations.recommendation:
Collect the amount of ₱780,500.00 representing the excessive contract costs of the rescue motor vehicle from the supplier and the ₱175,000.00 advance payment for lifetime warranty on service and parts, otherwise the subject amount will be disallowed in audit and shall hold all those who approved the payment thereof as liable.
Direct those who are in-charge of the inspection and evaluation of subject goods to exercise due care and diligence in the performance of their functions to avoid paying excessive contract cost.
observations.managementAction: Pertains to previous administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to create and maintain an official website to enhance transparency and enforce accountability pursuant to Sections 98 and 99 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2018.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the GSO to file an application for disposal of all unserviceable transportation equipment with the COA Audit Team following the procedures provided under Section 167, COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
Require the City Accountant to drop from the book of accounts the book value of the disposed property pursuant to Section 195 of same COA Circular.
observations.reason: No disposal of properties for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The City Mayor direct the City Housing Office (CHO) Department Head to locate the documents relative to the individual Contract to Sell/Memorandum of Agreement of the 165 home partners/beneficiaries of Bayanihan Habitat Homes.  In case the City has no available documents, coordinate with the Office of the Habitat for Humanity Philippines and request for aforesaid records.

Moreover, the CHO must conduct inspection on the resettlement project determine whether the 165 home partners are occupying the housing units.
observations.managementAction:
The Head of the City Housing Authority has sent letter to the Western Visayas Habitat for Humanity last December 21, 2018 asking for details on the payments made to them by the home partners.
Subsequent steps will be taken like ocular inspection, direct collection of the share of the city and updating the records.
observations.reason: Due to absence of records.

observations.recommendation:
The City Mayor assign the CHO to monitor the project, ensure that home partners comply with the Agreement and enforce the provisions of the agreement for those in default home partners. 

The OIC- City Accountant to recognize or record the receivables from home partners for the cost of the lot awarded to them once all the documents are established and send demand letters to all Bayanihan home partners with overdue amortization.
observations.managementAction:
The Head of the City Housing Authority has sent letter to the Western Visayas Habitat for Humanity last December 21, 2018 asking for details on the payments made to them by the home partners.
Subsequent steps will be taken like ocular inspection, direct collection of the share of the city and updating the records.
observations.reason: Due to absence of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor submit specific legal basis for the grant of Economic Welfare Assistance to the City officials and employees for CY 2017.  Henceforth, he must ensure that benefits or incentives granted to City officials and employees are expressly provided by law, and other statutory authority and the rules and regulations promulgated by competent authority pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 13, 2013.
observations.managementAction: No Management Action
observations.reason: The City continued giving other personnel benefits

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Submit documents or data that incentives and awards to each employee was based on performance, innovative ideas and exemplary behaviour.

b. Information or records that monetary awards was granted only when the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts resulted in monetary savings and payment did not exceed 20% of the savings generated.

c. Agency’s Annual Work and Financial Plan and budget showing that at least 5% of the HRD Funds allocated for the PRAISE was incorporated.

d. Management should carefully evaluate the validity of giving out incentives, especially the basis thereof and whether the City Officials and employees who were granted monetary incentives are qualified to receive the same.
observations.managementAction: No Management Action
observations.reason: The City continued giving other personnel benefits

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: AAPSI was submitted to the Audit Team on July 17, 2020 only.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the Audit Team’s previous recommendation that Management:

Require the BAC to promptly submit requests for contract review and technical inspection for completed projects to the COA to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection, and observe the necessity of compliance on the required documentary requirements for submission within the timeframe for review and inspection; and
Submit valid justification for our review and evaluation why the related payments to the said infrastructure projects should not be suspended in audit for failure to comply with the required submission.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Contract review for the nine infrastructure projects perfected in CY 2019 was not submitted to the Audit Team. Also, no request for technical inspection was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Submit comment from the Municipal Mayor, SB and Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) as to the grant of PEI despite the knowledge on the excess of PS limitation; and

Prospectively, faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 325 of R.A. No. 7160 and related DBM Budget Circular in the grant of PEI to avoid audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction:
The Audit Team maintained its position on the computation of the excess PS limitation based on actual PS expenditures of the LGU as reflected in the submitted FS.

Management commented that the fund used for the grant of the PEI was sourced from PS savings from vacant positions and suggested that based on their computations, excess in PS limitation amounted to ₱28,694.11 only. They also stated that a resolution will be enacted taking into consideration Budget Circular No. 2017-4.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Submit comment on the non-compliance with existing DepEd guidelines on the procurement of instructional materials; 
Prospectively, strictly comply with the provisions of DepEd Order No. 53, series of 2016 and other related issuances on the procurement of instructional materials; and
Prospectively, strictly comply with the provisions of R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Management was not able to submit comment.

Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management ensure that only expenditures that have direct bearing or falling within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities are included in the LDRRM plan and charged to the Fund. We likewise recommended that the LDRRM Council monitor and evaluate the use and disbursement of the LDRRMF as required.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, there were still expenditures charged to the fund that were not directly related to disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjustments to close the CIP accounts to its appropriate asset accounts. Further, allocate respective depreciation expense to present accurate property balances in the financial reports.

Likewise, municipal employees in-charge of project implementation should properly coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to regularly update the latter on CIP accounts.
observations.reason: Related finding incorporated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned official/employee to refund the excess payment of TA which the official is no longer entitled due to the use of a government vehicle; and
Ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents to establish the validity of claims.
observations.reason: Excess payment of TA was not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the four mandatory positions in the LDRRMO be filled up by permanent employees in accordance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM- Civil Service Commission (CSC) JMC No. 2014-1.
observations.reason: The designated LDRRMO was required by the CSC to comply with the one year actual experience to qualify for the position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and
Promptly implement all the PPAs as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.
observations.reason: Verification of SAAOB disclosed that 13 projects charged under the 20 per cent DF remained unfinished/ unimplemented as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency comply with the requirements of the SC Decision and return the PDAF released by DBM to the National Treasury to revert it to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the government.
observations.managementAction: A Notice of Disallowance (ND) was issued by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Management has written appeal to the COA Regional Director where they meticulously detailed their justifications centered on the dates and exemptions from the SC’s decision, that the GENSET intended for a cooperative in our town is not covered by the SC ruling.

observations.recommendation: Account for the missing units to clear them of their accountability.

observations.managementAction: A case had been filed with OMB-Visayas.

observations.reason: CCTV still not functional until now.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
Require the City Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of all City’s PPE so they could submit the complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
Require the City Accountant and the GSO to implement regular reconciliation of their records and undertake specific measures to eliminate the discrepancies between the two records; and
Require the City Accountant and the GSO to maintain updated PPE ledger and property cards, respectively so reconciliation of the two records could be done easily.

We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Accountant:
Enforce immediate settlement of all outstanding cash advances totaling ₱6,546,358.75, especially those intended for special purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-02 dated February 10, 1997;
Send   demand   letters to officials and employees who have long outstanding cash advances to settle the overdue amounts within a specific period; and
Otherwise, effect the withholding of   their   salaries   pursuant to Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 until such time that the cash advances are fully settled.

We recommended that the City Mayor and/or the City Accountant immediately send a follow up letter to COA-ICFAU to ascertain the status of previously submitted liquidation documents for charges against confidential fund for CYs 2004-2013 and request issuance of credit advice.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to:
Submit the cash advance vouchers for Confidential Fund together with the supporting documents within seven days after the release of the check to the Audit Team for post audit pursuant to Item 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015; and
Adhere to the regulation that cash advance for CF shall be limited for the three-month requirement pursuant to Item 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01; and the City Accountant agreed to adhere to the regulation that cash advance for CF shall be limited for the three-month requirement pursuant to Item 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Terminate the Job Order agreement between the City of Talisay and Ms. Tan because the fee paid to the latter constitutes double compensation considering that she is also an employee of the Province of Negros Occidental.
Follow strictly the provisions of Section 8 Article IX of the Philippine Constitution and Section 95 of the Local Government Code on the prohibition that no elective or appointive public officer or employee shall receive additional, double or indirect compensation, unless specifically authorized by law.

We recommended that the City Mayor propose for approval to the Sangguniang Panlungsod specific guidelines on the implementation of the Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation using as basis the guidelines issued by the DSWD under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 011.

We recommended that the City of Talisay submit specific law, other statutory authority, or rules and regulations of competent authority expressly authorizing such payment of additional benefit to its officials and employees pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 30, 2013. Otherwise, such payment could be considered wanting of legal basis.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Require the City Development Council (CDC) to prepare and include specific program, projects and activities that partakes the nature of investments and capital expenditures in the 20% Development Fund Plan pursuant to Item 2.3 of JMC No. 2017-01 dated                    February 22, 2017; and
Secure first approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to implementation of programs/ projects/activities funded out of the lumpsum appropriation under the 20% Development Fund pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2010-014.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) with the assistance of the City Environment and Natural Resources Office (CENRO) to ensure that the rules and regulations prescribed by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 on waste segregation be strictly enforced and violators penalized.
We also recommended that the City Mayor provide garbage receptacles for biodegradable and non-biodegradable solid wastes inside and outside the Public Market areas and assigned enforcement team who will monitor proper segregation and dumping of wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant and OIC – Municipal General Services Officer to give
priority to the reconciliation of the Municipality’s records and to effect the
necessary adjustments in the books or Inventory Reports for the noted
discrepancies;
b. Inventory Committee to prepare the Inventory reports using the prescribed
RPCPPE for the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type as of given date
and fully accomplished all the columns included therein;
c. Municipal General Services Office to have an updated list of those items for
disposal and incorporate it in the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), prepare all the necessary documents/reports
and act on the properties to be disposed within reasonable time; and
d. Review the all the items included in the PPE accounts and reclassify the
identified misclassified items totaling P1,711,940.00 to their proper PPE
accounts.

We recommended that the Management submit a written justification for non-
filling of application for disposal/divestment of Talisay Public Market as well as
that of other unserviceable property plant and equipment to the Commission.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the JEVs and their supporting documents which was used
as basis for drawing a JEVs for the derecognition for the said property plant and
equipment.

Henceforth, observe and comply with all the necessary requirements prior to the
demolition of its properties pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386 and PD No. 1445;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer
and the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Refrain from utilizing the current year’s appropriation to pay prior year’s
unrecorded obligation and to strictly adhere with existing laws, rules and
regulations governing the expenditures within the limits of the current
appropriations otherwise, transactions of the same nature in the future will
be disallowed in audit.
b. Ensure that the funds are exclusively used for the purpose for which they
were appropriated; and
c. See to it that all valid/lawful obligations are properly taken up in the
accounts of the year they were incurred, pursuant to Section 350 of RA No.
7160 and Sections 4(1) and 119 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Petty Cash Fund (PCF)
Custodian to:

a. Ensure that complete documentary requirements support the replenishments of
the PCF by using the prescribed record in recording the transactions and
making use of the PCV for disbursements out of the PCF;
b. Reduce the PCF of the AO equivalent to one month consumption only and/or
establish a realistic amount of PCF to ensure that funds are efficiently
budgeted; and
c. Apply the necessary accounting practices in consonance with Section 48 of
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Make necessary action to inform and demand the personnel about their
obligations to the municipality and cause the immediate refund/restitution of the
cash shortage and collection of the amount due from municipal officials and
employees representing unpaid personal telephone bills and bank loans;
b. Send demand letters to the listed debtors in order for them to settle their
obligation;
c. Initiate appropriate legal actions to recover from officials and
employees who were already separated from the service;
d. Intensify collections of Loans and Other Receivables, designate key personnel who
will monitor the loans/assistance granted and enforce payment thereof. Otherwise,
resort to appropriate legal remedies.
e. Coordinate with DepED personnel and exhaust all possible means to locate the
liquidation documents/records submitted by them in support to their unliquidated
cash advances.

We also recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed with the
Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016 - 005 dated
December 19, 2016 should collection be not feasible despite diligent efforts and after
exhausting all available remedies. Submission of all proofs that efforts have been
initiated to collect the same should be undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
adopt additional measures and strategies to intensify collection of taxes and
generate more income; coordinate with the barangays in the implementation of
Real Property Tax Program to maximize collection of taxes; and continuously
coordinate with the Provincial Government and Sangguniang Panlalawigan to
avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160 to deter delay and
non-payment of taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Release the retention money to the concerned suppliers in accordance
with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
b. Conduct a thorough review and analysis and ascertain the cause of the
negative and small amount of balances of this payable accounts and
effect the necessary reclassification or adjustments. Attach relevant
documents and analysis of the accounts to the JEVs for any adjustment to
be made;
c. Henceforth carefully check, analyze, review and promptly record the
transactions to arrive at an accurate and correct balances of the accounts
in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Officials to give priority and to effect the full payment of terminal
leave benefits of its retiring employees.
b. Municipal Accountant to use the proper accounts classification to record the
transactions correctly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Office-in-Charge to:

a. Implement measures to increase the income and decrease the expenses of the
Operation of Tourism to ensure that it will sufficiently meet or even exceed its
operating costs to help sustain the Municipality’s operation; and
b. Study the viability of this enterprise and submit plans for the improvement of its
collections as well as measures to decrease its expenses to ensure its
sustainability.

We recommended that the Management maximize the utilization of the 70% of
5% LDRRMF to enable the Municipality to implement projects related to disaster
prevention, mitigation and preparedness within the scheduled timeframe to
prevent possible loss of life, damage to government and private property, and
loss of livelihood.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer to ensure that
the LDRRMFIP contains the details of the projects and activities to be funded,
including those planned for the utilization of the previous year’s LDRRMF.

We recommended that the MDRRM Council:

a. Decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and review
and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs
to be funded by the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous
years so that the Municipality becomes a disaster resilient community; and
b. Accomplish completely the LDRRMFIP by providing sufficient information in
the listed PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. GAD Focal Person to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of
GAD Plan and Budget to ensure full utilization of the fund;
b. MPDO, with the assistance of the GAD Focal Point System to fully establish
the GAD Database and gather the necessary information for gender
analysis and sex-disaggregated data to facilitate evidence-based and gender-
responsive planning and policymaking.

Lastly, we recommended preparation of the annual GAD Accomplishment Report in
the required format for the proper evaluation of the implementation of GAD Plan and
Budget.

We reiterated our previous recommendations for the Management to:
Prepare and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the Auditor in prescribed format, not later than January 31 of each year. In the preparation of the report, the different column should be properly filled out to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for;
Perform periodic reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts; and
Give preferential attention on ensuring that parcels of land declared as owned by the Municipality are covered by Torrens Title/TCT duly issued by the Register of Deeds.

We reiterated our previous recommendations for the Management to:
Prepare and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the Auditor in prescribed format, not later than January 31 of each year. In the preparation of the report, the different column should be properly filled out to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for;
Perform periodic reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts; and
Give preferential attention on ensuring that parcels of land declared as owned by the Municipality are covered by Torrens Title/TCT duly issued by the Register of Deeds.

We recommended the Management to:
The Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer coordinate to determine the status of implementation and appropriate account classification of projects recorded in the CIP accounts;
Effect necessary adjustments to transfer the completed projects recorded from the CIP accounts to its appropriate asset accounts and allocate respective depreciation expense to present accurate property balances in the financial reports; and
Prospectively, perform periodic reconciliation of records from the Offices the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to enhance accuracy and reliability of reported CIP accounts balances.
We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to Paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.
We recommended the Management to reclassify the portion of loans payable that will mature within one year from Statement of Financial Position date from long-term to current liability.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on Infrastructure Assets of the Municipality in accordance with relevant regulations for fair presentation of the PPE accounts.

We commended the Management for its efforts in addressing the perennial recommendation on the preparation of BRS. On the other hand, we recommended the Management to:
In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant, determine the appropriate disposition on these transactions such as (a) in case the stale checks are still in the possession of the local treasury, adjustments in the books of the affected accounts or replacement of checks; and (b) in case the stale checks are in the hands of the payee, replacement of new checks upon compliance with the required procedures; and
Install a monitoring mechanism that will facilitate notification to payees or holder of checks issued one month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance.

We reiterated our previous recommendations for the Management to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other departments concerned to:
Promptly submit requests for contract review and technical inspection for completed projects to the COA to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection; and
Observe the necessity of compliance on the required documentary requirements for submission within the prescribed timeframe for review and inspection.

We reiterated our previous recommendations for the Management to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other departments concerned to:
Promptly submit requests for contract review and technical inspection for completed projects to the COA to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection; and
Observe the necessity of compliance on the required documentary requirements for submission within the prescribed timeframe for review and inspection.
We recommended the Management to direct the BAC to regularly prepare and submit the PMR in accordance with the provisions of R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and in the format prescribed by the GPPB.

We recommended the Management to:
Re-organize the composition of the LGU’s Project Monitoring Committee (PMC) taking into account members qualification requirements stated in DILG MC No. 2004-078; and
Conduct an orientation as to the functions and responsibilities of the PMCs to attain transparency in the implementation of their programs/projects and active participation of its members.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the withholding of 10 per cent retention money on all payments of infrastructure projects to cover the defects that may arise before the final acceptance; and
Instruct the BAC, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to require the contractors to submit/renew the warranty securities to ensure that the interest of the government is protected, in case of any structural defects or deficiencies that may arise after the final acceptance.

We recommended the Management to:
Ensure that POs are duly accomplished, with emphasis on filling out the corresponding dates for the following: (a) delivery, (b) signed by supplier and (c) signed by the Local Chief Executive; and
Submit valid justification on the inability of the Management to properly fill-out the POs.

We recommended the Management to:
Optimize the utilization of the 20 per cent LDF and implement the programs, activities and projects (PAPs) within the timelines in order to achieve the socio-economic and environmental outcomes, essential in the attainment of its goals and objectives of a desired quality of life for its constituents; and
Direct the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the development projects identified are feasible and can be implemented within the year with the proposed budget allocation.

We recommended the Management to ensure that only expenditures that have direct bearing or falling within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities are included in the LDRRM plan and charged to the Fund. We likewise recommended the replenishment of the amount paid for loan amortization charged to the LDRRMF to support disaster risk management activities.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare strictly the annual LDRRMFIP; and
Consider the inclusion of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended Quick Response Fund (QRF) and DRRMF-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the previous years which were transferred to the Trust Fund, in the preparation and review of LDRRMFIP, to help support the DRRM activities of the LDRRMC within the next five years.

We recommended the Management to:
Enhance monitoring of LDRRMF utilization focusing on PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of DRRM; and
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities are achieved.

We recommended the Management to:
Establish an internal policy or guideline in the hiring and file management of job orders and similar contracts, incorporating inputs from the Human Resource Management Office, Municipal Budget Office and other offices concerned; and
Review existing job order contracts with a view of harmonizing the agreed daily wage rates with the established internal policy.

We recommended the Management to identify the GAD PPAs through the use of HGDG which would be the basis in the budget determination depending on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD interventions.

We reiterated our previous recommendations for the Management to:
a. 	Direct the Accountant to immediately submit the unsubmitted CY 2019 Monthly Trial Balances and Quarterly FS and take appropriate immediate remedial measures such as augmenting the Municipal Accountant’s Office of personnel from other Departments, especially during report preparations and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of these mandatory financial reports, pursuant to mandated deadlines. Henceforth, to prepare and submit the same, with complete Schedules and Annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with the above-mentioned existing rules and regulations; and
Allow the Accountant to recommend qualified personnel of the Accounting Office to attend trainings or seminars relative to their works to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to improve their performance.

We recommended the Management to:
Observe prompt submission of DVs and ORs including the corresponding reports each month on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month; and
Strict adherence to the cited rules and regulations on the rendition of accounts and reports be enforced by the concerned officials in order not to incur penalty as provided in Section 122(2) of P.D. No. 1445.

The Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of BRS of all the accounts in the Banks of the LGU for CYs 2019 and submit them to the Audit Team, together with the corresponding JEVs/paid checks/original copies of Debit and Credit Memos for review and verification. Look into the possibility of using the snapshot of the Bank Statements as practicable under the circumstances, in preparing the BRS; and
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the Accountant’s Office of personnel by hiring additional qualified personnel to specifically prepare the monthly BRS and/or allowing the personnel in-charge of said BRS to render overtime, subject to availability of funds in order to submit the same to the Auditor on the mandated deadline.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Monthly CRAAFs accompanied by the respective RAAFs for the period January to June 30, 2019 and monthly thereafter in accordance with the MNGAS.

We recommended the Management to:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventory items of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balance of the said account in the financial statements.
We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to align the manner by which RPT shares are distributed with the provisions of the Local Government Code.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:
Immediately verify the under releases noted above and release the same to the Barangays in accordance with Section 271 of R.A. No. 7160; and
Reconcile the RPT shares reported in their books and records and effect necessary adjustments.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Treasurer to explain in writing the incurrence of negative balance of the Cash in Bank amounting to P52,120,621.89.
We also recommended that Management direct the City Treasurer to observe proper cash management to reduce the risk of inability to meet immediate cash requirements of the local government unit.

We recommended that Management require all transfer of funds to Barangays to implement the projects and programs of the City be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement and other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish proper accountability and monitoring of the proper utilization of the funds.
Moreover, require the officials of the barangays concerned for the immediate liquidation of the amounts granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the City.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Accountant to record in the individual employees’ ledgers the amount of contributions/deductions and reconcile the accounts and explain why the amount remitted differed from the amount withheld thus the incurrence of over remittance to GSIS and non- remittance to BIR, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth of material amounts.
Remit regularly all amounts due to the BIR within the prescribed time to avoid payment of penalties, surcharges and interest arising from violations and preclude delay in the processing of members’/employees claims and present the account correctly in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the City Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method for supplies and materials; and
Direct the General Services Officer to establish control procedures to regulate the issuance of supplies to actual requirements of end-users within a month.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct  the City Accountant to determine the cause of negative balances and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in order to correct the negative balances of the CIP account.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify the line items amounting to P121,551,422.44 to their proper account/special account classifications.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to take up in the agency’s books the total amount of P29,360,762.70 under Receivables-Disallowances/Charges account which is the total disallowance in COA Order of Execution (COE) No. 2015-179.

We recommended that the City Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Panlungsod, adopt and implement guidelines taking into consideration the following:
Review the amounts of cell card load allowance in relation to the available landlines/mobile phones and internet connection and consider its reduction.
The officials/employees who should be entitled to it and the amount one can claim for his official phone calls and any excess on the limit set, shall be for the personal account of the user.
If cell cards shall be allowed, consider the use of mobile phone fixed plan offered by telecommunications network.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the BAC to explain in writing why the Abstract of Canvass/Bids were signed by the canvassing officer and the head of the requesting department instead them; and
Require a written explanation from the canvassing officer and the head of the requesting department why the contracts were awarded before the deadline of the submission of bids/RFQs.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Schools Division Superintendent to submit the following:
Explanation/justification on the procurement of SRMs that are not included in the list of approved Supplementary Reading Materials;
Consolidated List of Requested SRMs of the Division pursuant to Item 3 of DepEd Order No. 53 which shall be supported by the following:
School Inventory of SRMs
List of usage of new SRMs needed by the school
How the SRMs that will be procured will support the curriculum implementation; and
Approval of the request from the Office of the DepEd Secretary.
b. Direct the BAC to submit an explanation on why the procurements of SRMs were made without considering the provisions of DepEd Order No. 53 s. 2016.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the DepEd – Talisay City Division to explain in writing why the specifications of the books requested for the Kindergarten and Grade 1 pupils did not comply with the DepEd DO No. 16, s.2016; and
Review carefully the submitted Purchase Requests from various end-users to avoid unnecessary expenditures of public funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to:
a. Trace and identify the causes of the unreconciled balances of the reciprocal accounts and effect their reconciliation; and
b. Maintain and update regularly the subsidiary ledgers of the said accounts to facilitate early detection of errors, and determine if the total of the individual accounts tally with the general ledger.

We recommended that Management use the e-NGAS because of the aforementioned features which are not available in the system currently being used by the Accounting Office.
We also recommended that Management:
Hire encoders, preferably with Accounting background, to speed up the data build-up and beginning balances, as well as the encoding of transactions to the System, until a parallel test shall have been conducted; and
Provide the Accounting Office with a separate server to ensure the efficiency of the System and to safeguard its database.

We recommended and Management agreed to establish an Internal Audit Service office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445 in order to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, improve the quality and quantity of outputs and enable agencies to better respond to the requirements of the public they serve.

We recommended that Management avail of the cash card services offered by the Development Bank of the Philippines or Landbank of the Philippines in the payroll distribution of the job order workers or formulate and implement strategies to minimize the waiting time of the job order workers by observing a schedule for the distribution of the salary per office.

We recommended that Management direct the Sangguniang Panlungsod and the Office of the City Assessor to have proper coordination to resolve the impasse regarding the general revision of the real property assessments in order to update the assessed value of the real property within the City’s jurisdiction and consequently increase its revenue/income.

We recommended that Management:
Make a demand to CSWMI to comply with the material provisions in the contract in order to establish a controlled sanitary landfill, otherwise, rescind the contract; and
Direct the Ecological Solid Waste Management Office to make a regular monitoring on the actual implementation of the contract.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Reconsider the JVA and determine whether the failure of CSWMI in the performance of its obligations constitutes material breach in the agreement and pursue necessary legal actions; and
Compel CSMI to perform its obligations, if warranted, under the JVA in order for the City to receive the benefits therefrom.
We recommended that Management install CCTV cameras in the City Treasurer’s Office to deter robberies, theft, and irregularities of cash and cash items thus safeguarding the assets of the agency.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the City Engineer to submit a justification and explanation why the contract was reported as 100% complete despite the noted variance;
Remind and require the City Engineer to be more circumspect in the inspection of actual accomplishment of projects considering that it is his mandated duty to administer, coordinate, supervise, and control the construction, maintenance, improvement, and repair of roads, bridges, and other engineering and public works projects of the local government unit as provided in Section 477 of the Local Government Code; and
Require the contractor to correct the noted deficiency; otherwise, settle the deficiency in the amount of ₱10,758.00 as it constitutes irregular expenditure, hence disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to achieve completeness of documentary requirements, economy and effectiveness in the implementation of infrastructure projects and to avoid suspension and eventual disallowance of the transaction.
We also recommended that Management submit the above specified documentary requirements or a written explanation why such could not be submitted.
We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of the LGC and to transfer/realign any existing appropriations charged to the LAFF in the SP Office to the budget of the Mayor’s Office.

We recommended that Management facilitate the completion of the 10-year solid waste management plan.  We also recommended that, Management comply with all applicable rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing the open dump site, develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and eventually, residual wastes of the City.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer religiously submit to the COA auditor, copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO, the annual LDRRMFIP and monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund certified correct by the City Accountant pursuant to items 5.1.5 and 5/1/6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to create two plantilla positions for a) administrative and training; b) research and planning and hire qualified personnel to occupy these positions following the qualification standards, position titles and salary grades set by NDRRMC, DIL, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
