We recommended and Management agreed to submit the LDRRMFIP prepared in accordance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2, 2012.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
	
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all government properties  and  submit  the  Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year pursuant  to  Section 124  of  the  MNGAS  for  LGUs, Volume  I so the Audit Team has ample time to validate the reported balances of PPE.
Require the Inventory Committee to locate the whereabouts of motorcycles reported as unserviceable in the RPCPPE but were not found at the City Motor Pool.
Require the City Accountant-Designate and the GSO to implement the immediate reconciliation of their records inasmuch as the discrepancy affects or involves not only motor vehicles and other transportation equipment but applies also to other PPE accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.
Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City strictly comply with the requirements of Section 37 of RA No. 9003 to convert open dumpsite into controlled dumpsite.  In addition, the City Mayor directs the City Official to expedite the construction of a sanitary landfill as final disposal of residual wastes and adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological wastes management program and encourage waste reduction/reuse/recycling and composting as strategies to reduce the solid wastes.

Moreover, the City include in the Annual Budget as well as in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) the procurement of a brand new bulldozer or compactor to be stationed at the dumpsite so that a heavy equipment is readily available to push large volume of solid wastes for levelling and compacting wastes discharged from dump trucks.
observations.managementAction: The Sanitary Landfill of the city government is on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Officials to fast track the submission of the requirements in securing the Environmental Compliance Certificate to facilitate the construction of the one-cell Sanitary Landfill to address the problem of the City on waste management.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City officials who are liable for the disallowance to settle the amount of disallowance immediately to the Cashier.  Further, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the Cashier/Treasurer to withhold payment of any money due such persons liable pursuant to Sections 3, Rule XIII of the 2009 Revised Rules and Procedures of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the City Development Council (CDC) ensure that projects to be funded by the 20% Development Fund (DF) adhere to the guidelines under Item 3.0 of Joint Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We further recommended that the City Budget Office and the City Accounting Office properly evaluate expenses chargeable against the 20% DF and must discontinue charging expenses to the 20% DF that do not partake of the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Accountant-Designate to immediately transfer the unutilized prior years’ balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the STF under the Trust Fund books pursuant to Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-12 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction Council (CDRRMC) promptly decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF which shall be incorporated in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) pursuant to Item 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-012 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Balances of LDRRMF were gradually transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Local Finance Committee composed of the City Planning and Development Coordinator, City Budget Officer and the City Treasurer to properly determine the income reasonably projected as collectible for the ensuing fiscal year, recommend appropriate tax and other revenue measures considered realistic and feasible to support all planned projects, programs and activities included in the Annual Budget pursuant to Section 318 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Mayor/City Development Council:
Direct all implementing offices to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects and activities, particularly, the construction of drainage and flood control program and the purchase of dump trucks so that the desired benefits from these projects could be delivered to the constituents.

Identify specific development projects to be implemented prior to the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and indicate the definite timelines of implementation so that these projects could be implemented without the need of the SP approval.
Instruct the CPDO and the CEO to monitor the physical status of implementation/completion of the projects.  A monthly report shall be prepared by them on the monthly status of the projects furnishing a copy to the Audit Team.

The City School Board stop the practice of charging expense not authorized under section 272 of the Local Government Code and Item 4.0 of Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 dated January 18, 2017 against the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The City government has minimally stopped the charging of expenses not authorized under section 272 and Item 4. 0 of JC No. 1 series of 2017

observations.recommendation: Moreover, the City School Board must comply and consider all the requirements in the planning and preparation of the SEF budget, ensure that all expenditure items to be included therein shall be among those provided Section 4.0,shall be net of the budgetary provision for the same or related items in the budget of the DepEd and ECCD Council and contained in the approved School Improvement Plan (SIP) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDP), pursuant to Section 5.0 and 5.2.1 of Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board review and update the Solid Waste Management Plan to ensure sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.

We recommended that the two parties (City of Talisay and the 3rd District Representative) enhance the MOU regarding the Integrated Burial Assistance Program or the PROGRAM by indicating the specific amount of fund that each party will contribute, the estimated number of beneficiaries, including their specific duties and responsibilities.  Moreover, it is recommended that the counterpart fund including the City’s share shall be recorded as Trust Fund to be used only for the specified purpose.
We also recommended that the City Mayor implement the program once the MOU is amended and the terms and conditions are clearly defined.
observations.managementAction: The City Officials have already discussed the issue with the staff and officials of the Representative of the 3rd District of Negros Occidental.
observations.reason: No amendment of the MOU was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office maximize the utilization of the prior years’ LDRRMF and the current fund so it could serve the purpose of equipping the City against the adverse impact of hazards and related disasters.

We recommended that management should demand payments of the loans granted to cooperatives and closely monitor compliance of their commitments, otherwise, institute legal action to collect the same, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to farmers’ cooperative
observations.reason: Some Farmers’ Cooperatives were paying their loans already.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all small value assets with useful life of more than one year but costing less than P10,000.00 are properly classified and recorded accordingly and to prepare journal entries and effect the necessary adjustments on the PPE, Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts for a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; b. Require the General Services Officer (GSO) to issue the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to all the end-users of the small tangible items and to cancel the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) previously issued; c. Require the GSO to take immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the abovementioned unserviceable properties as required by PD No. 1445 to prevent exposure to different elements and further deterioration of its values; and d. Require the creation of a Committee to reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPEs with the property and accounting records.
observations.managementAction:
a. Municipal Journal entries have been effected to make the necessary adjustments on the PPE.
b. ICS have been issued to all the end users of the small tangible items and the ARE have been cancelled.
c. Appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties is yet to be conducted. GSO inventory report and no IIRUP was submitted to the Audit Team.
d. None
observations.reason: Appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties is yet to be conducted. GSO inventory report and no IIRUP was submitted to the Audit Team. Composition of Committee is yet to be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to: a. Refrain from utilizing the current year’s appropriation to pay prior year’s unrecorded obligation and to strictly adhere with existing laws, rules and regulations governing the expenditures within the limits of the current appropriations otherwise, transactions of the same nature in the future will be disallowed in audit. b. Ensure that the funds are exclusively used for the purpose for which they were appropriated; and c. See to it that all valid/lawful obligations are properly taken up in the accounts of the year they were incurred, pursuant to Section 350 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 4(1) and 119 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: According to the Management, prior year’s transactions were not recorded as Accounts Payable due to the lack of documents presented in the Accounting Office. A Sangguniang Bayan Resolution was passed authorizing the charging of those expenses to the current year’s appropriation.
observations.reason: Similar observation was observed in CY 2019. Reiterated in finding no. 3.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the: a. stop the practice of giving financial assistance directly to the request of school/school heads, consolidate the utilization of the fund strictly for its intended purpose and the same may be charged to the SEF, procurement of which should be through the proper mode of procurement under the RA No. 9184; b. require the DepED and the barangay officials to issue Official Receipt/Accountable Forms 51 for every financial assistance provided by the municipality in the disbursement of funds; c. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe/follow updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009; d. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary reclassification and adjustments in the General Fund book for the transactions pertaining to the misclassified accounts in order to fairly present the financial statements; and e. strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 305(b) of RA No. 7160, and develop a system of monitoring on the use of the financial assistance to ensure that the funds are spent for the purpose for which it was intended.
observations.managementAction: All the Offices and the management agreed that the informed that disbursement through reimbursement basis will not be allowed. DepED and barangay officials assured the conduct of procurement instead, procurement under the RA 9184; The updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 is being adhered to by the Management. Proper classification is applied in CY 2019. Other Professional Services assistance charged to the municipality in the disbursement of funds; The Municipality requires the recipients to submit the liquidation reports and other related documents from recipients. Certification from DepED Accountant that the named School Heads did not claim/reimburse the registration fee and travelling expenses for the said training and other documents to validate the proper utilization of the funds to ensure that all disbursements are properly and completely documented, otherwise these will be suspended/disallowed in audit; The Municipality ensured the completeness of the supporting documents as required under Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing the payment; and the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the MPDO to strictly and closely monitor the execution of the project is in accordance with the timeliness set in the contract.
observations.reason: There are still payments with lacking supporting documents. Some supporting documents were still noted in the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to, in coordination with the Municipal Human Resource and Management (HRM) Office to: a. regularly reconcile the balances in accounting records with GSIS records in order to ascertain the reliability of the amount Due to GSIS in the financial statements; and b. effect timely and accurate loans and remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and contributions.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Offices have met with GSIS for reconciliation of Due to GSIS in the records with the records. GSIS Premiums were timely remitted.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), as the other Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to: recommend the appropriate method of procurement and encourage competitive bidding instead of alternative modes, when the circumstances allow, and avoid splitting of purchases to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and to obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government. We also recommended that the management ensure that all the supporting documents for all disbursements are duly and properly filled-up.
observations.managementAction: Procurements are based on the Bids mode of procurement. The Accounting Office required the municipal officials/employee and claimants the submission and proper supporting documents for all disbursements made. The Municipality adheres to the procurement plan.
observations.reason: This observation is included in the Special Audit of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents as required under Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing the payment; and b. Municipal Engineer in coordination with the MPDO to strictly and closely monitor all projects and ensure that execution of the project is in accordance with the timeliness set in the contract.
observations.managementAction: All necessary documents are still being prepared. The Municipality is working on the completion of the supporting documents. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer, who released the check to the contractor, did not transmit the said vouchers to the Accounting Office. Additional deficiencies were noted by the Construction Technical Audit Specialists upon ocular inspection of the Construction of the New Municipal Hall Building.
observations.reason: This observation is included in the Special Audit of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure the conduct of pre-procurement conference before the Procuring Entity start with the procurement process; b. BAC and BAC Technical Working Group (TWG) to provide an explanation for the inconsistencies in the different phases of the procurement process; ensure at all times that winning bidders post performance securities within the prescribed period to ensure interests of the government are adequately covered in the event a winning bidder fails to perform; require the winning bidders to submit performance securities with a validity period as well as require the contractor to submit corresponding extension of the security’s validity period if there are contract time extensions; and be more cautious in evaluating the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by bidders during bid evaluation and post-qualification; c. BAC Secretariat to ensure the posting of warranty securities in the payment of infrastructure projects by the contractors and demand for liquidation of damages from erring contractors for delayed completion of work and to submit all the lacking documents immediately to the Audit Team to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit; and d. Municipal Accountant to submit a written justification on disbursing government funds for infrastructure projects despite insufficient supporting documents and submit immediately all the lacking documents presented in Annex L.3 to the Audit Team to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and other Municipal Officials assured the conduct of pre-procurement conference before the Procuring Entity start with the procurement process; Submission of performance securities with a validity period is now being required to the winning bidders. Submission of performance securities is being evaluated. The Municipal Accountant submitted written justifications to the Audit Team. The Municipality is in the process of submitting the lacking supporting documents. Additional deficiencies presented in Annex L.3 are being required to the bidders. The Municipal Engineer, MPDO and Municipal Accountant are monitoring the ongoing projects.
observations.reason: There are still incomplete supporting documents. This observation is included in the Special Audit of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and TWG to: a. scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower and equipment on all bidders to be evaluated; b. strictly evaluate during bid evaluation and post qualification the List of All Ongoing Government and Private Contracts and whether or not the submitted Net Financial Contracting Capacity (NFCC) of the bidders who were awarded several projects accurately reflect its financial capacity to carry out the work sought to be undertaken; and c. monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies and to coordinate with those involved in implementing agencies and to prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to the Honorable Municipal Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors.
observations.managementAction: Technical and financial documents submitted by all bidders are being evaluated. All infrastructure projects are now being monitored by the Municipal Engineer. Random monitoring is being done on some contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies. The Municipal Engineer and MPDO monitor projects simultaneously implemented by contractors and prepare status reports for monitoring purposes.
observations.reason: There are still few overlapping implementation periods noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the BAC to recommend the appropriate method of procurement and encourage competitive bidding instead of alternative modes, when the circumstances allow, and avoid splitting of purchases to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and to obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government. We also recommended that the management ensure that all supporting documents for all disbursements are duly and properly filled-up.
observations.managementAction: Procurements are now based on the Bids mode of procurement. The Accounting Office required municipal officials/employees and claimants to submit the supporting documents and properly filled-up documents before processing their claims.
observations.reason: This observation is included in the Special Audit of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the proper application of fidelity bonds for all accountable officers and ensure that the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality is covered by fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Proper application of bonding for all accountable officers is now being observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer to: a. ensure that the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness as presented in the LDRRMFIP contains the details of the projects and activities to be funded, including those planned for the utilization of the previous year’s LDRRMF; b. meticulously plan the specific programs and projects necessary in the prevention, mitigation and preparedness of the Municipality in case of calamity and ensure the immediate implementation; and c. carefully scrutinize the expenses that should be charged to the fund to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP contains the details of the projects and activities to be funded, including those planned for utilization of the previous year’s LDRRMF as required under Section 6.2 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1. The Municipality ensures timely implementation of the specific programs and projects. The Municipality verifies the expenses charged to the fund to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Audit noted non-optimum use of the LDRRMF for CY 2019 as per finding no. 11 page no. 56.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. GAD Focal Person to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to ensure full utilization of the fund; b. MPDO, with the assistance of the GAD Focal Point System to fully establish the GAD Database and gather the necessary information for gender analysis and sex-disaggregated data to facilitate evidence-based and gender-responsive planning and policymaking. Lastly, preparation of the annual GAD Accomplishment Report in the required format for the proper evaluation of the implementation of GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: Some steps were undertaken to establish/update GAD Database and maintenance of sex-disaggregated data is still on-going. Implementation of GAD activities are regularly monitored. However, some GAD issues prioritized during the year were not supported with baseline data and some GAD activities were not implemented.
observations.reason: Some GAD issues prioritized during the year were not supported with baseline data; GAD Database maintenance is still on-going; deficiencies were noted in the submitted GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Direct the Accountant to immediately submit the unsubmitted CY 2018 Monthly Trial Balances and Quarterly FS and take appropriate immediate remedial measures such as augmenting the Municipal Accountant’s Office of personnel from other Departments, especially during report preparations and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of these mandatory financial reports, pursuant to mandated deadlines.  Henceforth, to prepare and submit the same, with complete Schedules and Annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with the above-mentioned existing rules and regulations; and
Allow the Accountant to recommend qualified personnel of the Accounting Office to attend trainings or seminars relative to their works, to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to improve their performance.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action: 

Observe prompt submission of DVs and ORs including the corresponding reports each month on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month; and
Strict adherence to the cited rules and regulations on the rendition of accounts and reports be enforced by the concerned officials in order not to incur penalty as provided in Section 122 (2) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: There were still DVs not submitted for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Instruct the inventory committee to furnish the Auditor a copy of the inventory report of the physical count; and
Also, direct the Municipal Accountant and designated property personnel to maintain complete and updated PPE subsidiary records and conduct regular reconciliation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Related observation incorporated in Part II of this report and AAR CYs 2016 and 2017.
observations.reason: Some account titles used were not in accordance with the revised chart of accounts, hence, hindered the reconciliation of PPE account balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management take the following courses of action:

Prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate the timely review of the sources of fund, ascertain the validity of expenditures and to determine whether the programs, projects and activities were included in the LDRRMFP; and
Maintain separate RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO that will serve as monitoring tool in determining the accuracy of balances and the amount that will be transferred to Special Trust Fund; and submit the same to the Audit Team for review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Submit the GAD Plan and Budget as well as the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team for evaluation; and
Management should strictly comply with the provisions of the JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA to ensure that proper planning and budgeting is provided for the GAD program and activities which will address the gender related issues and concerns identified in the GAD plan.
observations.managementAction: Related findings incorporated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Not yet fully adhered to.
The reports were on file, however, these were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of actions:

Execute lease contracts for the market space and stall holders and impose the conditions set forth in the said contracts; and
Observe the posting of the Notice Time for Collection of Tax and List of Delinquent Taxpayers to each of the 15 Barangays of the Municipality as required by R.A. No. 7160.
observations.reason:
The Management were still updating list contract of market space and stall holder.
The posting of the Notice Time for Collection of Tax and List of Delinquent Taxpayers was not updated to some Barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Prioritize the preparation and submission of the Monthly BRS to ascertain the reconciling items pertaining to the difference amounting to ₱1.89 million in order to establish the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts for all funds as required in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; and
Necessary adjustments, if any, should be affected to avoid accumulation thereof and to present the correct balance of the cash in bank accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: On-going preparation of monthly BRS. | Delay in the preparation and submission of BRS due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of records to establish a realistic balance of Receivables from RPT and SET so that necessary adjustment(s) in the books of accounts can be effected for fair presentation of balances in the FS; and
The Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to determine correct and complete information used in the computation of real property tax.

observations.managementAction:
On-going reconciliation. Related finding incorporated in Part II of this report.

Management stressed that despite repeated verbal and written demands from dismissed employees of the LGU, they were not able to surrender/ turn-over the PPEs previously issued to them.

The Municipal Accountant ensure that no new cash advances were allowed to accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances.

Management sent demand letters to Accountable Officers who are no longer connected with the Municipality.

Cash advances for travel are no longer allowed to accountable officers with outstanding cash advances. Travelling expenses were paid through reimbursement to avoid further accumulation of cash advances.

Accountable officers and employees will be informed of the recommendation to enforce the settlement of their accounts.
observations.reason: As of June 30, 2020, cash advances amounting to ₱859,896.73 remained outstanding/ unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council select/consider strictly PPAs that would be charged to the 20 per cent DF in accordance with the mandate of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and

Promptly implement all the PPAs as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: There were still expenditures charged under the 20 per cent DF that were not related and/or not connected with the implementation of development projects for CY 2019.

observations.managementAction:
Management averred that utilization of SEF were strictly in accordance with the SEF Budget.

Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Absence of the three-year strategic program and recommendation of the DepEd Public School District Supervisor (PSDS) and Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) in the planning and budgeting for the SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Transfer all the unexpended QRF and Mitigation Fund (MF)-MOOE to a Special Trust Fund while all unobligated balance of LDRRMF for Capital Outlay (CO) should be made continuing in the General Fund until the projects funded therefore are completed in accordance with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided under COA Circular No 2012-002;
Prepare and certify the correctness of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and the LDRRM Officer and submit the same to the identified offices on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Also, the LDRRM Officers should be cautious in complying to the posting requirements of such reports in conspicuous places including websites, if any, of the LGU/LDRRMO; and
Observe strictly the requirement set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically Section 5.1.16 on the proper and adequate disclosure/discussion in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the amounts and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of using appropriations for donation other than the purpose for which it was intended for.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Ensure that all radio communication equipment owned/used by the Municipality have pertinent permits such as permit to purchase and license to operate in compliance with the NTC Radio Law;
All radio communication equipment should be purchased from accredited radio communications equipment dealer or manufacturer with a valid Radio Equipment Dealer’s or Manufacturer’s Permit or an entity or corporation whether private or government with a valid Permit to Sell only; and
Furnish the Audit Team the lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: The necessary documents were only presented in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management install measures to facilitate the timely submission of transaction documents from the MTO to MAO, and ultimately to the Audit Team, within the first 10 days of the ensuing month.

Henceforth, Management should see to it that Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1 paragraph (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and be strictly complied, by close coordination and follow-ups with the concerned responsible officers/employees.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

Management exert more efforts to cause the settlement of the cash advances from former official/employees who were transferred, retired or separated from service;
Municipal Accountant document the request for write-off for account balances which were considered dormant and where collectability could no longer be ascertained pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
Municipal Mayor file request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to former official/employees.
observations.reason:
Documents for attachment for request to write-off dormant accounts were not secured.
No request for authority to write-off dormant accounts filed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than their official travelling expenses to adhere strictly to the rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as required under Section 339 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
Ensure that DVs and other supporting documents are properly signed by authorize signatories; and
Monitor liquidation of the cash advances and verify the liquidation voucher/report and supporting documents before recording in the books of the agency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant constantly reminds AOs of the liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.reason: Liquidation of cash advances were made beyond the required period of liquidation deterring proper monitoring of cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee and appraise the market value of the unserviceable properties;
Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in COA Circular No. 95-006 and COA Circular No. 89-296 which are either through (a) public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by destruction; and
Facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties to recoup any salvage value thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management is still securing the necessary requirements for disposal of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: Unserviceable properties remained undisposed as of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend the Management to pay for the penalty charged to government funds due to delayed remittances must be immediately recouped from the person responsible for the late remittance.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to submit the monthly list of attendance to sessions for SB members and Daily Time Records (DTRs) for department heads prior to the preparation of payroll;
Strictly observe the grant of RATA and the prescribed schedule of rates in accordance with Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 to avoid overpayment of the same; and
Require the concerned officials to refund the excess RATA claimed amounting to ₱100,800.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant constantly reminds AOs of the liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.reason: No refund of the excess RATA claimed by elected officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 prescribing the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions and to verify the completeness of documents before recording utilization of funds in the books of accounts to establish the validity and propriety of claims;
Management conform to the prescribed allowable rates of per diems and transportation expenses for travel in accordance with E.O. No. 298 as amended and COA Circular No. 96-004; and
Management ensure the most economical fare should be availed for travelling expenses and avoid accompanied travels that are unnecessary considering its cost and benefit.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce the existing regulation pertaining to salary deductions and adopt control measures by not permitting any additional loan payments to be deducted on the salaries of those who are receiving minimal net take home pay, to ensure compliance with the General Provisions of the GAA for CY 2016.

observations.managementAction:
Management averred that database are being maintained.

Related finding incorporated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: No documents submitted to verify compliance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LGU should coordinate with PAG-IBIG and request for offsetting of the excess employer’s share remitted in CY 2015 against the succeeding payments.
observations.managementAction: Management averred that the employer’s mandatory contribution is no longer exceeding the allowable employer share of ₱100.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the 55 per cent PS limitation in accordance with R.A. No. 7160. Disbursements should be limited to the amount of appropriation as approved and reviewed in the annual budget otherwise overpayments shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management: 

Ensure prioritization of expenses that needs to be included in the budget; and 
Direct the MBO to stop obligating expenditures without an approved ordinance in compliance to Section 4 (1) of P.D. No. 1445 to avoid incurrence of illegal expenditures.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

The agency institute internal rules to regulate the grant of mobile load allowance considering rank, cost and amount of expenses chargeable to government funds to preclude the incurrence of excessive mobile phone expenses.  It is imperative to establish rules and regulations for the allocation of mobile phone allowance to avoid wastage and abuse of government resources; 
The LCE should see to it that scarce resources are managed well and to exercise prudence in the implementation of programs and projects like a good father of a family; and 
Avail of unlimited call and text services from telecommunication companies to maximize use of municipal funds.
observations.managementAction:
An SB Resolution was submitted providing monthly communication allowance to Municipal Government Elected Officials, Department Heads, Assistant Department Heads and Designated/OIC Department Heads and other officers of the LGU-Talisay effective January 1, 2014.

Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Proper planning and monitoring of programs, projects and activities to ensure that resources are managed well.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure availability of cash before obligations are incurred so as to comply with the modified accrual basis of accounting for complete reporting of all financial transactions, and all prior year’s obligations charged in current year’s appropriation should be recorded as prior period adjustments for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant, MBO and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the procedures on the disbursement of government funds; and
Ensure that DVs are properly signed by the concerned Head of Unit and approved by the LCE before payments of checks are issued.
observations.managementAction: Management said that at this moment, they see to it that all disbursements are properly authorized and approved.
observations.reason: Some of the officials concerned were on travel and others might have overlooked it due to voluminous transactions, hence, some of the DVs were not completely signed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LCE appoint immediately the lacking members of the LDRRMC in compliance with R.A. No. 10121 to achieve the purpose of promoting and strengthening partnership with all sectors and all stakeholders concerned towards complementation of resources and effective delivery of disaster risk reduction programs, activities and services to citizenry.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued memorandum to concerned offices.
observations.reason: Copy of the new executive order for the composition of the LDRRMC was not furnished to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management comply with the 55 per cent PS limitation in accordance with R.A. No. 7160. Disbursements should be limited to the amount of appropriation in the annual budget as approved and reviewed, otherwise overpayments shall be disallowed in audit.

Refund the amount of ₱2.75 million representing payment for Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI).
observations.reason: No refund of the PEI made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop payments thru reimbursement process in the acquisition of supplies, materials, catering services and other miscellaneous expenses and to adhere strictly to the policies mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: Management asserted that reimbursement for acquisition of supplies, materials and other miscellaneous expenses was already discontinued.
observations.reason: RCIs showed that there were still reimbursements made for various expenses in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor should instruct the concerned municipal employees to adhere to the provisions set forth in the COA Circular No. 2012-001 regarding procurement;
The Municipal Accountant should reclassify the accounts in the proper inventory account; and
Require the MDRRM Officer that the distribution of relief goods be always provided by a validated distribution list.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE issued a memorandum directing the concerned officials to comply with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit all collections due to other government agencies and ensure the timely remittance of all on the ensuing year.

We recommended that:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the use of perpetual inventory method in the recording and issuance of drugs and medicines including medical, dental/laboratory supplies;
Refrain from dropping inventory items from the books of accounts without adequate documentation; and
Require the concerned Property Officer to submit the Summary of Supplies/ Materials Issued (SSMI) and its supporting documents for the drugs, medicines, medical, dental/laboratory supplies to the Accounting Division for recording the expense through the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
Management stressed that using perpetual inventory method is an exercise in futility as the supplies are very limited and often not enough for the constituents.
No SSMI submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer requires the drivers to properly fill up and accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTTs) before the approving officer signs them. Likewise, adequate control should be established to ensure that expenditures on fuel and lubricants be limited to the minimum and avoid wasteful consumption.
observations.managementAction: Management averred that DTTs were already properly accomplished.
observations.reason: Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Report of Official Travels were not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant requires complete documentation to all disbursement vouchers before payments are made in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reminds all concerned officers and employees of the submission of complete documentation of all disbursements.

observations.recommendation: Restitution of the amount by the recipient public officials as appearing in the payroll purporting to the payments for PEI or Additional Bonus for CY 2011.
observations.managementAction: Officers and Employees refund the amount received in installment basis.
observations.reason: Not yet fully refunded/returned.

observations.recommendation: Direct Management that the aforementioned PAPs be sourced from funds allotted for Gender and Awareness Development, Calamity Fund, 1% IRA for activities and programs for the protection of children, and other sources.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management expedite the rescission of the Contract of Lease in order for the City Government to cause the projects to become operational, generate income and provide jobs to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The contract has been rescinded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant that periodic reconciliation of the Cash in Bank Account be performed pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to establish the accuracy of the balance of said accounts. Likewise, effect the necessary adjustments on the unrecorded cash deposit and interest income.

Moreover, prepare a letter requesting the Manager, LBP Osmeña Branch and DBP, Talisay Branch that the City Government be provided copies of its monthly bank statements thru email/online so these can be viewed/downloaded on time.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the requisitioning officer on the procurement of drugs and medicines to attach a certification that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition in compliance with Paragraph II.2 of DOH AO. No. 163 s. 2002.
observations.managementAction: The procurement of drugs was supported with the necessary documents.
The Management already created an Inventory Committee to undertake the reconciliation of the records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and submit the subsidiary schedules supporting the general ledger balances and to maintain subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared and submitted the subsidiary ledgers of the accounts.
Management submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government of Talisay execute a yearly contract of lease embodied in a public instrument for every public market stall rental and follow the standards/guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 88-282A to ensure that interests of both parties are protected, ambiguities and/or misunderstandings can be avoided.
observations.managementAction: The contracts were already submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the BAC to include in the Terms of Reference the expected output/deliverables of the consultancy contract in order to properly quantify the progress and the extent of the services rendered by the consultants; and

Require the hired consultants to submit their PhilGEPS registration number.
observations.managementAction: The PhilGEPS registration numbers of the consultants were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC to give an explanation and justification of the splitting of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required documents in order to support the validity and propriety of the expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the City Engineer to submit a justification and explanation why the contracts were reported as 100% complete despite the noted variances in the road concreting as compared with the program of work;

Remind and require the City Engineer to be more circumspect in the inspection of actual accomplishment of projects considering that it is his mandated duty to administer, coordinate, supervise, and control the construction, maintenance, improvement, and repair of roads, bridges, and other engineering and public works projects of the local government unit; and

Require the contractors to settle the deficiencies in the amount of P600,521.61 as it constitutes irregular expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The contractors refunded the disallowed amounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Local School Board (LSB) to conduct the proper planning of the procurement activities in order to carry out the priority projects and activities chargeable to the SEF; and

Require the DepEd – Talisay City Division to submit the SIP and DEDP for the year 2018 in order to determine whether or not the expenditures charged against the SEF are net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the LSB formulate a three-year program for the strategic allocation of the SEF. Likewise, submit the Local Board Resolutions for CY 2018, School Improvement Plans and Division Education Development Plan for auditorial review.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the SIP and DEDP for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the LSB Council incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for ECCD pursuant to Republic Act No. 10410 and DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The SEF Budget for CY 2019 incorporated the funds intended for the ECCD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the city’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) prepare the GAD accomplishment report in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-001 so that proper monitoring and performance evaluation of GAD related projects/programs and activities can be made.
observations.managementAction: The GAD accomplishment report was already submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
ensure that PAPs funded by LDRRM will support the disaster risk reduction and management activities, during the pre-disaster preparedness program and post-disaster activities; and
hasten the implementation of all PPAs under LRMMDF to prevent possible loss of life, damage to properties and loss of livelihood.
observations.managementAction: The fund was substantially utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and  Management agreed to:

a. Direct the LDRRMC to properly identify the PAPs to support the disaster risk reduction and management activities, during the pre-disaster preparedness program and post-disaster activities; and

b. Identify the PAPs for the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF together with the current year’s allocation to ensure its efficient utilization.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to integrate the PPAs to be sourced from the LDRRMF unexpended balances of prior years to the AIP.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor require the LSB to facilitate and ensure the full implementation of priority projects as embodied in the Annual Budget and identify the reasons for the non-implementation of the programs/projects/activities.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to immediately stop the practice of using the SEF outside the scope for which this fund was established and observe the proper utilization thereof pursuant to the provisions of DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 and Section 100(c) and 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.
The NGOs submitted copies of Deeds of Undertaking to pay monthly for the funds which they could not account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to remit all Philhealth contributions especially those pertaining to prior years to avoid payment of interest/penalties, preclude delay in the processing of members’/employees’ claims and present the account correctly in the financial statements.  Moreso, we recommend that Management direct the City Accountant to explain why the amount remitted differed from the amount withheld.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Accounting Section to speed up reconciliation of accounts to establish the correct balance or unremitted portion and avoid incurrence of interest for delayed remittance and present the account correctly in the financial statements. We also recommend that the City impose penal sanctions on the officials or employees responsible for the non-remittance of contributions or payments due the Home Development Mutual Fund or the PAG-IBIG Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and Management agreed to direct the Accounting Section to reconcile the accounts, explain the incurrence of negative balances and remit whatever balance due to the GSIS to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and to enable the member beneficiaries of the City to avail of benefits from the GSIS, thus fairly present the account balance in the financial statements of the agency as of a given period.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management remit regularly all taxes withheld to the BIR within the prescribed time to avoid payment of penalties, surcharges and interest arising from violations and submit justifications relative thereto.  Henceforth, we recommend that the City strictly implement the remittance of taxes withheld on time in compliance with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to remit all PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth contributions especially those pertaining to prior years to avoid payment of interest/penalties, preclude delay in the processing of members’/employees’ claims and present the account correctly in the financial statements. We also recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to explain why the amount remitted differed from the amount withheld and to reconcile each employee’s records and ledger to determine the completeness of the contributions withheld from them.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and  Management agreed to: 

Direct the City Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method for supplies and materials; and

Assign staff to establish control procedures to regulate the issuance of supplies to actual requirements of end-users within a month.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Section to reconcile the accounts, explain the incurrence of negative balances and remit whatever balance due to the GSIS to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and to enable the member beneficiaries of the City to avail of benefits from the GSIS, thus fairly present the account balance in the financial statements of the agency as of a given period. Further, we recommended that Management shall see to it that it has fully remitted monthly the employees’ and government shares.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that City Solid Waste Management Plan be prepared as required under Section 16 of RA 9003 for purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management. Also, immediate creation of Barangay SWM Committee in all remaining barangays is recommended.

Moreover, to ensure efficiency and effectiveness of solid waste management program, the following were recommended:

Separation of collection for biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste pursuant to Section 21 of RA 9003.

Conversion of the City’s controlled dumpsite into an Eco-Park with Residual Containment Area (RCA) or sanitary landfill facility pursuant to Section 37 of the same RA.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory taking of all the PPE items to ascertain the actual existence and condition of the properties and equipment. The committee may be composed of personnel from Accounting and General Services Office to work together to reconcile both the inventory records and the property ledger cards.
direct the City Accountant to re-classify unserviceable properties to Other Assets awaiting for their disposal if there are any based on the inventory report of the committee;
exert efforts to locate the missing properties and make a demand to the end users for the return of the missing items.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.
Management has started to work on the titling of the lots but discrepancies in the documents have delayed the processing of titles.

observations.recommendation:
Collect from the retired personnel their outstanding liabilities;
Require the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable including those who signed the clearance of retired personnel in satisfaction of the disallowances or charges as mandated under COA Circular 2009-006.

Management is still in the process of migrating data from the old system to the eNGAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the different market collectors to prepare, on a periodic basis, the Report on Unpaid Stall Rentals and submit to the City Treasurer/Accountant for monitoring/recording  purposes; and

Require the City Accountant that the total realizable income from market stall rental fees is properly taken up in the books of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendations.
Management promised to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the City Treasurer to employ more aggressive collection strategies such as tax mapping, monitoring of weights, calibration of fuel meters, and other means to increase collections.

Establish an Internal Audit Service office and install a sound system of internal control to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, improve the quality and quantity of outputs and enable agencies to better respond to the requirements of the public they serve in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The City Mayor direct the City Housing Office (CHO) Department Head to locate the documents relative to the individual Contract to Sell/Memorandum of Agreement of the 165 home partners/beneficiaries of Bayanihan Habitat Homes.  In case the City has no available documents, coordinate with the Office of the Habitat for Humanity Philippines and request for aforesaid records.


Moreover, the CHO must conduct inspection on the resettlement project determine whether the 165 home partners are occupying the housing units.

The City Mayor assign the CHO to monitor the project, ensure that home partners comply with the Agreement and enforce the provisions of the agreement for those in default home partners. 

The OIC- City Accountant to recognize or record the receivables from home partners for the cost of the lot awarded to them once all the documents are established and send demand letters to all Bayanihan home partners with overdue amortization.

The Talisay City Local Management Council which was tasked and was given authority to implement and administer WVHH programs and projects and to keep records was dissolved with no proper turn-over of records.  On the part of the WVHH there were changes of staff and personnel.

Demand from NOccHFH the remittance of all lot payments directly paid by the home partners to its office. Moreover, request for the return of all remittances made by the City representing lot payments withhold from the employees’ salaries.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY
2017 | The Head of the City Housing Authority has sent letter to the Western Visayas Habitat for Humanity last December 21, 2018 asking for details on the payments made to them by the home partners.
Subsequent steps will be taken like ocular inspection, direct collection of the share of the city and updating the records.
observations.reason:
Due to absence of records.

The Talisay City Local Management Council which was tasked and was given authority to implement and administer WVHH programs and projects and to keep records was dissolved with no proper turn-over of records.  On the part of the WVHH there were changes of staff and personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City strictly comply with the requirements of Section 1 Rule XIII of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9003 to convert open dump into controlled dump.  
In addition, the City must expedite the construction of a sanitary landfill as final disposal of residual waste and to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological waste management program and encourage waste reduction/reuse/recycling and composting as strategies to reduce the solid wastes.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY
2017 | The Sanitary Landfill of the city government is on process.
observations.reason:
The progress of building the SLF was stopped due to some ECC requirements.
Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor submit specific legal basis for the grant of Economic Welfare Assistance to the City officials and employees for CY 2017.  Henceforth, he must ensure that benefits or incentives granted to City officials and employees are expressly provided by law, and other statutory authority and the rules and regulations promulgated by competent authority pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 13, 2013.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Submit documents or data that incentives and awards to each employee was based on performance, innovative ideas and exemplary behaviour.

b. Information or records that monetary awards was granted only when the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts resulted in monetary savings and payment did not exceed 20% of the savings generated.

c. Agency’s Annual Work and Financial Plan and budget showing that at least 5% of the HRD Funds allocated for the PRAISE was incorporated.

d. Management should carefully evaluate the validity of giving out incentives, especially the basis thereof and whether the City Officials and employees who were granted monetary incentives are qualified to receive the same.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY 2016 | AAR
CY 2016 | No Management Action | No Management Action
observations.reason: Not Implemented | The City continued giving other personnel benefits
